Name: Moneyball
Review: I have never watched a baseball game in my life yet this movie has given me the feel of watching a baseball tournament.... this movie holds the suspense in the scene and deliver it right through and makes this movie a great watch for the sportsdrama lovers .... Moneyball is a fascinating portrayal of a revolutionary approach to baseball. It is a great blend of the sports-drama and marketing strategy. Overall, this is a must-watch for sports enthusiasts. If you are a sportsdrama lover never miss this movie!!!

Name: Secret
Review:
An incredibly beautiful tale of love which is perfectly blended with the music and romance.

The first half is too slow but the second half gets you highly engaged with the film. 
P.S: you will never expect such a plot twist.

Though it is a slow burn in the beginning, but once you complete this, you will never forget this masterpiece.

Name: The Notebook
Review:
The Notebook is a spell that mesmerises you with it's tear-jerking emotion and enduring love.

Halfway through the film: "They are like shooting stars, A spectacular moment of light in heavens, A fleeting glimpse of eternity. And in a flash they are gone."

By the End: Love is like the day and night, they fade in and fade out but never end.

Name: Mujhse Dosti Karoge!
Review:
Has a good plotline, but lacks character development.Just another Bollywood cliche with a manipulative male lead and his victims... 
Why does people consider it a happy ending 🤕🚶🏻‍♂️🚶🏻‍♂️. Better avoid this movie.

Atleast soundtrack was good 😌.

Name: Merry Christmas
Review:
Merry Christmas is a gripping thriller that keeps you hooked up with its twist and turns...This film is musical ride of suspense and dark comedy.

What else do you need when you have sriram raghavan in the film!?

Name: Aavesham
Review:
In one word it's "Dopamine overdose 💉💉" Ranga is not just a "Character" it's a "vibe🕺🏻". It's been two days since I watched the film and yet I ain't out of it. These phrases keeps getting on my head.
"Eda mone happy aano!?" "Nannayi vaa!!!"

Name: Damsel
Review: Predictable scenes but an overall good watch ‼️

Name: Dil Chahta Hai
Review:
Dil chahta hai is quite a great movie which has a good plotlines and a character depth for the main characters in the movie.
The movie feels like a great biriyani which has all the flavours blended perfectly where the flavour of the movies are the playfulness of the youth, love, friendship and self-discovery. It's a must watch for the fans of feel good and heartfelt cinema lovers.

Name: Lost Ladies
Review:
"Laapata Ladies" is an engaging film with an unique story premise. This film keeps the audience entertained with its humour while subtly addressing the societal norms and gender inequality. The performances by the lead actors is excellent, retaining the originality and the depth to their character. 
It's a good watch for those who like thought provoking and entertaining cinema.

Name: Lighting Up the Stars
Review:
"Lighting up the stars " is a heartwarming drama film that explores the shades of loss, redemption and unexpected relationships. The actors in the pivotal role played their part so good, that our heart feels emotionally attached to their characters.
The movie is on point, staging the moments of sadness following by a heartwarming scenes showcasing the beauty of human relationships. The film's score is subtle yet impactful,  complementing emotional depth of the story.
Overall this is a heart touching film that reminds us of the beauty of the unexpected relationships and bondings. This is a definite must watch film for feel good movie lovers.

Name: Tune in for Love
Review: "Tune in for love" is a charming love story that is connected through the the elements of time and destiny.But the pacing and the repetitive plot stops it from reaching its full potential. It is an enjoyable watch for romantic drama lovers.

Name: Forgotten
Review:
"Forgotten" is a gripping psychological thriller which keeps the audience hooked right to the film from the beginning till the end. The actor who played the main role (Jin seok) delivered an impressive performance, conveying the state of confused mentality and piling up  tension in him, thus adding the complex nature of his character.

Although you could see a twist coming halfway the film, you would never expect these many plot twists, which makes the film more engaging.
Though these twists make the film good, few people may feel fatigue of the piling up twists.(Too many twist n turns)

This is a must watch for those who love twist n turn movies. And a good watch for an casual movie lovers.

Name: Anonymously Yours
Review: The film's premise feels so fresh and familiar at the same time 🫠🫠. one time watchable movie.

Name: Raayan
Review:
"Raayan" is an engaging film which is packed with the elements of suspence, drama and action. The most impressive things about raayan is the commendable performances by the lead actors. Though the performances is at top notch, Most of the supporting characters weren't developed properly, making them a undercooked characters. The chemistry between the characters doesn't work good bcuz of its weak writing. The inconsistency in the chemistry makes the emotional scenes flat surfaced. 
The song "water packet" wasn't good for me while listening for the first time, but the placement and the way it is executed in the film makes it a enjoyable one and provides a better version of the song.
The tension and the drama seems to be well developed in halfway thru the film, yet the second half fails to extract the essence of the well developed drama of the first half.

Overall "Raayan" is a film that has well staged action packed scenes,and a strong lead performances. This could be a decent watch for the general audience and may be a good watch for the fans of its genre.

Name: Kal Ho Naa Ho
Review: In a movie where Srk is a lead, I liked the 2nd fiddle character (Saif) more. This movie is potrays two main characters with extremely contrary characteristics. Rohit (Saif) is a supportive, kind-hearted, RESPECTFUL, and genuinely cares for Naina (Preity Zinta). On the other hand, Aman (SRK) despite being the hero, has lots of red flag traits... Being too nosy on other's personal lives, emotionally manipulative, doesn't let other to state their opinion, makes chauvinist jokes on women. Despite of having these red flag traits, he is still considered the angel in disguise. Naina having such a green flag (Rohit) besides her, Yet she falls for the red flag (Aman)😭😭. This movie is still holds sadness as much as fun it has in it.

Name: A Clockwork Orange
Review:
I thought I was mentally strong until I watched this film 🫠🫠. This film is literally traumatizing af 😭😭. Now I understand why this film is highly censored or banned at so many countries. 

Disclaimer: This film has lots of violence and sexual assault scenes. Watch at the risk of your own mental peace!!!

Name: The Pursuit of Happyness
Review:
"The pursuit of happyness" is an inspirational drama film and It's a must watch film for those who are in need of some motivation in life. Happy tears are flowing out of my eyes everytime I see Will smith walking out of the office after being hired....
"This part of my life, this little part is called happyness 😭😭😭😭"

Name: Anbe Sivam
Review:
அன்பின் பாதை சேர்ந்தவனுக்கு முடிவே இல்லையடா... மனதின் நீளம் எதுவோ அதுவே வாழ்வின் நீளமடா... அன்பே சிவம்‼️
A heart that shreds tears for a stranger... That's god. Every Human has a god in them. Love is god.

Name: The Green Mile
Review:
"The Green Mile" is more than just a movie. It's one of the tear-jerker masterpieces that leaves a lasting impressions on your mind even after watching the film. This movie doesn't have much of a character arc but a great character depth. John (played by Micheal Clarke Duncan) is like a child with a pure heart and the characters around him represent the ordinary human characteristics and traits. The performances by the lead actors is too good, especially Tom hanks and Micheal Clarke Duncan. Though I tried to held back my tears...
When John said "Don't put me in the dark. I'm afraid of the dark" the moments before his execution...
I paused the film (I hate pausing while watching movies) and cried until my eyes drained out of tears.

Name: Where the Tracks End
Review:
"where the track ends" is a feel good film that removes the weights from your heart while watching it. The children group from the movie reminded me of my childhood days. The performances by the actors was subtle yet convincing which made the film feel so natural. Though this film conveys a strong message on "the importance of education" still feels so light hearted.
I don't know about the livelihood in rural Mexico... But this film convinced me to believe this is how the livelihood of a rural part of Mexico be.
Overall this film feels so authentic and highly recommended for the people who love light hearted movies.

Name: Sanju
Review:
Engaging, Inspiring, emotional, hopeful, empathetic, optimistic,.... I guess there words aren't enough to express my feelings... I never thought that a movie would give me these much mixed emotions at a time.... I literally feel very optimistic watching the film.... The issue handled in the film is too sensitive and complex.... Yet Rajkumar hirani managed to pull that off with his brilliant control over his craft... He made a great job on making the drug addict part of the film... And the 2nd half deals with the sensitive and complex issues,still he managed to keep that simple N clean and paced that part with emotions to make the audience feel into Sanju's character. The main pillars of the film is RR.
Rajkumar hirani and Ranbir kapoor
Ranbir Kapoor's acting is on whole another level that you can't imagine anyother actor to play that role. He just nailed it‼️ The one n half star is cut for oversimplifying the complexities and the controversial topics... Other than that this film is too good to be a rollercoaster of emotions sprinkled with fun throughout the ride.

Name: Drawing Closer
Review:
"Drawing Closer" is not just about drawing closer, but drifting apart too. This is the Japanese version of "the fault in our stars", "Dil bechara". The letter at the end lasted for almost 15mins and still felt so beautiful🤌🏻✨ I couldn't have cried anymore😭 3 gerberas for this film 😭🖤🤍
Someone please teach this man "Takahiro Miko" how to make a film without making the audience cry ‼️

Name: Hitch
Review:
RomComs makes your mood better. And "Hitch" could be one of them which makes the viewer feel light for a while with its own humour sense and the essense of love.
life is not the moments of your breath. It's the moment that takes your breath away.

Name: I Want to Eat Your Pancreas
Review: "I want to eat your pancreas" is a romantic movie which is pleasant for your eyes to watch but not for your heart. There is no way you gonna leave without a heavy heart watching this film.

Name: Planes, Trains and Automobiles
Review:
The most disastrous trips can be the most memorable and enjoyable one. You start to cry busting out laughs and by the end you cry cause of the emotions (Those aren't sad).
"I would like you to come over for Thanksgiving." This scene was too good.
Overall this is a must watch for those who like comedy films and feel good movies. This film is a classic peice.

Name: A Clockwork Orange
Review: Life was good until I decided to re-watch this film.

Name: The Dictator
Review:
This film is definitely aladeen. An overly crude and offensive jokes, but they are still hilarious.
Doctor: Do You want the Aladeen news or the Aladeen news? Patient: The Aladeen news. Doctor: You are tested HIV-Aladeen. Patient: 😊😐😭😐😊(Confusion of emotions)

Name: Thangalaan
Review:
This film highly depends upon the performances of the actors in it and almost all the actors played their role convincingly(Vikram, Malavika and Parvathi's performance are stunningly good). 
Though the actors played their role well, the film feels so distant and could connect to the emotions in the film, except a few scenes during the song miniki miniki and the scenes of malavika mohanan as aarathi. The voices that Vikram whispers aren't clear cuz of the live dubbing and I couldn't get that properly. There are some other things that I can't understand but I ain't interested to know that cuz this film felt emotionally distant.
The story is predictable and after the first half the story is set in a single flat space without any high and downs with repeating plotlines which makes us feel fatigue of the lengthiness of the film. 
In my opinion with all the points considered Thangalaan could be an one time watchable film.

Name: Iyarkai
Review: Watching for the nth time and still the ending hits hard.

Name: No Hard Feelings
Review:
Guess how could the movie turned out if the roles were reversed 💀💀. 

By the way it's an regular film with a cliche premise but stands apart cuz of its good humourous scenes.

Name: Saripodhaa Sanivaaram
Review: "Saripodhaa Sanivaaram" isn't just any regular masala film. A simple social vigilante film which becomes a well written film with the interesting additional plotlines added to the story. Vivek Athreya has done a good job converting the script into a engaging film. The onething that I liked the most in the film is the breathing space between the highpoint moments. It is refreshing to see a stillness the scene which allows the audience to get to understand the emotional weightage of a scenes and resonate with the character's feelings. The breathing space made the emotional scenes become more impactful which inturn made the film to be more engaging and interesting.

Name: Premam
Review:
It's been 9 years since the release of the film and it's still feels enjoyable and refreshing to watch the film.
"Love is like a feeling,it can't be explained."

Name: Meiyazhagan
Review: "Meiyazhagan" is an emotional drama film which binds the themes of love, loss, and redemption. This film is a journey of two totally opposite characters narrated in a simplistic way, thus making the viewers to engage with the characters and their lifestyle. The most beautiful thing about the film is the emotional depth in the story which makes the character well developed. This character arc makes the character's own struggles more relatable and impactful. The most common opinion by the netizens about the film is that "the first half is good, yet second half doesn't work good and there was a lag in its pacing." But the second half is my favourite. The first half is liked by everyone because of its humourous and detailed storytelling and the establishment of new characters one after another. But the second half is totally contrast to the first half, the story totally revolves around the two character in the second half, fully filled with conversation of two men discussing about their life and vibing for "ILAYARAJA'S songs". There were some scenes about Jallikattu and Sterlite issues which is not relevant to the context, but as the second half is about character development, all those irrelevant scenes increase the details in characters and makes a better impact to enhance the climax scene. Overall "Meiyazhagan" is a wholesome heartfelt film that which combines simplicity and character driven storytelling and creates a lasting impressions on the viewers. It is a must watch for those who like simplistic stories that are relatable.

Name: Johnny
Review:
Can't believe this film is more than 40 years old !!! "Jhonny" is a gem from a 80s era of tamil cinema that stands out for its unique storytelling and great emotional connection. The storytelling in the film is mixed up ("intricate") and straightforward at the same time creating a gripping narration that keeps the viewers engaged. The most beautiful part of the film is the character development. The characters is the film are grey shaded individuals (neither bad nor good) with their own flaws, desires and relationship. As they confront thier personal chalenges, they evlove through their experience, Thus creating a authentic journey throughout the film. This film is filled with a lot of memorable and sharp dialogues. These dialogues expresses the chracter's idealogies and philosophy towards life adding an extra depth towards the narration. I feel that the portrayal of the love in the film is matured. It's fresh to see the bonding between the characters are developed through the conversations (the conversations are realistic) rather than some sort of physical activities.
RAJINI: Ama... Aarambathula padapada nu pesitingale!! Ena pathi thapa adhu idhu nu ennenamo nenachitingale... Naan apdi la nenaikuravana!? En apdila pesitinga!? SRIDEVI: Naan apdi dhaan pesuven (childish) RAJINI: En en!? RAJA'S masterpiece bgm rolls Sridevi nods head and both looks into eachother's eyes and starts to smile then slowly laughs.
This has become one of my most favourite love proposal scenes.

Name: Even If This Love Disappears from the World Tonight
Review:
"Even if the love disappears from the world tonight" is a gentle reminder that a true love is timeless and an enduring moment of magic. The story unfolds like a beautifully written love letter that captures the human emotions.
"Even if this love disappears from the world tonight, my heart will paint you."
I think this director (Takahiro Miki) doesn't know how to make a happy ending. This film had all the possibilities to be a happy ending, but just because he is in this film, it is made as a heartbreaking film 😭😭.
Engaging but predictable.

Name: A Silent Voice: The Movie
Review:
The words-unsaid bears the most pains.
I promise... I will not kill myself 😭😭.

Name: Vaazhai
Review: There is always a great respect for the film that shows the truth. The connection with the audience is the most important. For example, when we feel the fear in a particular scene, how we audience relate to that becomes crucial. The emotions from those genuine experiences deeply affects the audience. It's rare to see such portrayal of emotions in a film. "Vaazhai" is one of the rare film of this kind.

Name: The Intern
Review: "The Intern" is a feel good film that is led by the strong performance of Robert De Niro as a 70 year-old charming intern and Anne Hathaway as a commited workaholic women. The story revolves around how the life of this workaholic women is changed after the arrival of the 70 year old intern. It also reminds us that the value of the experience never fades. This film is refreshing and enjoyable watch for people of different age groups. PS: I'm slowing becoming a fan of the films that portrays "unexpected friendship".

Name: Vettaiyan
Review:
Vettaiyan is an action drama film that is made engaging with its plot rather than milking the starpower.

There were few mass scenes but they aren't on par for the superstar film's expectations and they aren't enough for the stardom of the man "RAJINIKANTH". Though this film has a good plot, the mass scene placements in the middle of the plot makes the plot to be diluted and the mass scenes too doesn't work good.

TBH I felt like the director is in a confused state of mind whether to make a mass masala film with superstar or a plot driven film. It could have been a even better film if the director made a clear decision about his way of presenting the film.

There was a character arc given to RAJINI's character and Fahadh faasil's role as battery was good and the scenes with him on the screen is really enjoyable.

Overall Vettaiyan is an engaging onetime watchable film.

Name: Meiyazhagan
Review:
Just me crying over again and again watching men having conversations and crying 😭❤️‍🩹.
Karthi saying: "En peru solli kuda kupda maataaru... Periyavan vandhutana!?... Periyavan saptana!?... En mela oru periya nambikai... Namma apdi la onnum illa nu namaku theriyum... Aana Amma kita adikadiku solluvaram.... Kavala padatha endha prechana irundhaalum periyavan paathukuvan..." I cried for this scene everytime I watched this 😭😭

Name: The Holiday
Review: Women written by Nancy Meyars has layers in their character.

Name: Lubber Pandhu
Review:
Tamilarasan Pachamuthu has hit a boundary in the first ball that he faced.
This is how a film turns to be when the relationships between each characters are written well. There were moments of buildups for the character with a voice over which is convincingly portrayed with the scene following it. Retro songs are used to elevate the characters and those did their job perfectly and made the scene more impactful. The performances of the lead actors are just on the mark, establishing their character's traits. 
The plot also focuses on the discrimination and equality, but they way they portrayed that in the film is very subtle and they don't discuss about this at most of the scenes and yet it is impactful bythe end.
Overall this is a great watch for both who expect commercial aspects and well written films.

Name: The Grudge
Review: There were moments of horror sequences struggling to convey the terror but they doesn't seem to be working.

Name: The Substance
Review:
Most of them feel "The Substance" to be a disturbing film, but I'm stunned and left with a silence in my heart and thoughts in my mind cause of its astonishing concept. 
The performances of Demi Moore and Margaret Qually is top notch that you can't even imagine anyone else on their character. They literally lived as their roles.
The film starts as a regular sci-fi film with its innovative idea, but as the plot progress they capture the essence of the human mentality, existential crisis, and self centric behavior of human. There progress made the characters feel more real even though the concept is surreal. This increases the emotional depth of the story, making the audience to relate with their characters. There is a character played by Dennis Quaid, he represents the film industry and the people who stereotypically sexualize  to define a beauty of a person.

The cinematography and the sound Design in the film is absolutely perfect!!! The way they used the environmental noises around the character to enhance the scenes.

"The Substance" offers a disturbing body horror, with its thrillingly fascinating plot. This is a must watch for those who expect to experience something fresh and new(disturbing too).

Name: Black
Review:
Is it my fault that I already watched coherence (2013)!?

The film "Black" has taken the oneline of the coherence and added an Indian-ish texture to the script. It is a well made remake of "Coherence". This film has a slow start and takes its time to get onto the mainplot. Once it gets into the mainplot, the film gets more interesting and engaging to watch.

Overall "black" is a decent watch for those who expect a well written sci-fi tamil film.

Disclaimer: Don't use headphones to watch this film, The sound thats used in few scenes provides an eerie vibe when played on surround speaker, but may make you feel discomfort when heard thru headphones.

Name: Devara: Part 1
Review:
Jr NTR's character and anirudh's music totally shoulders the first half. The second half felt like a new different story following the other Jr NTR character. The second half starts to lose all its built up stakes with the irritating love portions, highly specialized heroine character, and the slow paced high bass voiced dialogues of the protagonist and his crew. The stakes slowly starts to build again towards the climax and a twist is placed (though it was predictable, the film becomes engaging). They tried to pull off a rajamouli kind of play in the end, but it all felt flat and not necessary.

PS: The only things that I don't understand till now is that why are the police even listening to the story of devara🤔⁉️ (don't they have a task for them to complete).

Name: Sathi Leelavathi
Review: KH-BaluMahendra and KH-Crazy Mohan combo never dissapoints you. As expected the film "Sathi leelavathi" keeps you entertained to the core from its start to the end. Especially the last 20 mins are blasts of laughter. Kamal Hassan and Kovai Sarala deserves a great round of applause. The only thing that felt bad is the portrayal of Heera's character. Her roles is portrayed in two contrast way in both the half. At the first half she is just a normal lady portrayed in a positive light, but a the story progresses to the second half, just because they wanted to give a nice character arc to Arun (Ramesh Aravind), Priya is characterised as a cruel woman who is after the money of Arun. You don't have to slut shame a woman to justify the action of your male antagonist.

Most of them said that the film sound was jarring while watching in theater whereas for me it felt loud but not jarring.

Name: Rocky
Review: Rocky is one of the best sports drama that I have ever watched. This film really lives to the quote "HIS WHOLE LIFE WAS A MILLION-TO-ONE-SHOT". The way that the film progresses gets yourself into the film and makes you motivated and offers you rays of hopes. Watching the film, I got too interested and read about the BTS and the development of the film. Reading it, I see every last bit of their hardwork being reflected on the screen.

Name: Mirchi
Review: "Mirchi" feels like a feast that offers you everything, that is needed for a mass entertainer film.

Name: Viduthalai: Part I
Review:
Watched the director's cut as a recap of the part 1 and the director's cut is really amazing.
With just 5 mins of additional sequences, the detailings in the characters are highly elevated and the backstory (kumaresan vs OC) becomes more strong. With every details, the film feels more impactful and gets you into its world.

Although VJS character is shown at the end of the film, the impact that character carries is enormous.

Name: Pushpa 2 - The Rule
Review:
Although I tend to grow some hate towards the film for its overhype on the internet, this film impressed me on several occasions. 
I have set my mind that I gonna watch a telegu masala film, still some sequences are bit too much (illogical). I felt like the organic nature of the film is left out and adapted with unwanted elements to make it pan Indian (capture the Hindi market).

FAFA in pushpa the rise 🗿
FAFA in pushpa the rule 🤡

Name: Pogumidam Vegu Thooramillai
Review:
The film "pogumidam vegu dhooramillai" is starts as a regular film, but stands apart with its great writing. Vimal and karunas played their role perfectly.
The irony between the character of karunas and Vimal, and the usage of the karunas's dialogue as metaphor at the end of the film is way too good and hit me hard. 
The stories that revolves around a single night have impressed me a lot recently and "pogumidam vegu dhooramillai" is one of them.

Heartfelt movie for real ‼️
When Adam sees "facing cancer together" book in Adam's bathroom, my eyes were in tears.
Just because you don't express your love doesn't mean you don't care.

Name: Viduthalai: Part II
Review:
"Viduthalai part 2" is an ideology said with multiple propagandas and threads. 
It doesn't justify it's prequel as a film. It focuses more on its ideology than storytelling and giving viewer a satisfaction of watching a good film. I don't wanna argue about the ideology discussed in the film, but all I needed was to watch a good film but vetrimaran dissapointed me this time. 
Vetrimaran focused more on the socially, political rightness inthisfilm and could deliver an great film with the short duration of a film. It would be better if it is released as a webseries so he could have conveyed all the messages that he wanted to say and provide a good cinematic experience.

It felt like sitting in a social studies class rather than a movie theatre.

Name: The Song of Sparrows
Review:
"The song of Sparrow" is a film that resonates with the viewer on a whole another level. The beauty of the film lies in the way it portrayed the innocence of childhood, and the livelyhood of an ordinary family man. I personally loved the metaphorical representation of the connection between the lost ostrich and the father (Karim). They both were lost at first and found their way back home as the film progressed.
This film perfectly captures the essence of the life and serves as a ray of hope if you are looking for one.

It doesn't matter whether you are a fan of world cinema or looking for a simple heartfelt story, this is a must watch for you.

Name: Hope
Review:
Time: 5:56 AM - 30 JAN 2025 I have made a mistake by watching this film as a first job in the morning. Don't you dare to think that this film is a regular feel good film by seeing the title. Even if the film kinda feel good by the end, believe me, the journey you go thru for that is a hell, and make sure you are strong hearted before watching this film. Time: 5:22 PM - 30 JAN 2025 It's been half a day since I watched the film, but still I don't know what to say. I'm totally devastated by this film. I felt a lot of emotions while watching the film, anger, furious, disgusted, and disturbed. Time: 7:30 PM - 30 JAN 2025 Finally, now I could pull off myself to write something. This is not just a film that you decide to watch on weekend to chill. Hope is a film that will make you cry throughout the it. The tears that you shread are of pain, sorrow and rage and still the film manages to transend them into a ray of hope by its end. The following are the lines from the soundtrack of the film that impressed me a lot.
Hope, there is hope. Never be sad again ! trust in love that abounds...

Review:

Edhedho padatha re-release panringale.... Indha padatha re-release pannunga da...

Don't know why but ilayaRAJA sounded different from his regular style in this film and that was good.

Name: Kudumbasthan
Review: I would never forget the experience that this film gave me. I had an unforgettable theater experience today, when I found myself as the only person in the entire cinema. For the first five minutes, I was shocked, but then I embraced the solitude and realized that it is my time to enjoy the film however I wanted, without any interruptions. Then I sat in my seat comfortably and enjoyed the movie, laughing out loud at every comedic scene. Unlike many films where comedy feels forced, this movie's humor was genuinely situational based, which made it even more enjoyable. The comedy scenes in the end of the film is so epic that I cried laughing. The film follows a serious storyline, yet manages to deliver every scenes with a comedic touch, making it impossible not to laugh every five minutes. This is both a strength and a slight drawback. Just towards the film's end, there's an emotional scene that (where Manikandan breakdown and cry) is well made but lacks the space it needed, as the comedy scenes that follows quickly shifted the mood, diluting the emotional impact of the previous scene. Despite this, the film is exceptionally good and feels refreshing. Manikandan has once again proved that he is an actor with a great caliber and delivered a top notch performance. Kudos 👏🏻 👏🏻 to the entire team and the debut director Rajeshwar Kalisamy for making such a wonderful film in the first attempt. BTW andha Lesa Lesa song tribute nalla irundhudhu 🌝😌👍🏻

Name: Dragon
Review:
The highest form of appreciation that a grown man crave-for is being recognised by his father. We all know how this film is gonna turn in the end, but still this film manages to keep to engaged and entertained till the end. I see many people comparing this to "DON (2023)" but the character's arc in this film is better than that in the Don itself. It is portrayed in such a way that you can notice the step by step progress of the dragon character's transformation. The things that I felt like a negatives in this films are... The usage of profanity by the lead's friends gang. They are presented in a humourous way and they don't even say it directly but the profanity is implied indirectly. Though it was comical, there was multiple scenes like that and few scenes felt like the profanity is forcefully added element to cover the youngster audience. Anither negative is that, the beginning of the kutty dragon scenes felt repetitive and irritating for me, but after few scenes it doesn't feel like one and that character blends into the flow of the film helps in the development of the title hero "DRAGON". The emotional scenes at the end worked too well to me and
This segment is a spoiler* I was literally crying when his dad accepts his son's mistakes and feels proud that his son has now realised his wrongful acts.
This is a well written script that is presented in a pakka commercial way with the comical tone of the film leading in the front.
"Adhu ungaluku sonna puriyadhu sir" Indha scene ku whole theater eh summa blast aagiduchi 💥💥
DRAGON: 4/5⭐ (3.5 for the film and 0.5 star for my co-audience at the theater)

Name: (500) Days of Summer
Review: I stand with Tom on first watch and I gonna rewatch this again today. Link for the re-watch's review

Name: (500) Days of Summer
Review:
On re-watching the film, i realised that summer always had a clear mindset and never gave mixed signals to Tom. It was Tom who lived in his own imaginary world thinking that she is into him and he was always self-centred and can't accept the fact that the relationship is a two sided coin.

There are also few instances that I felt Summer was wrong, one is when summer goes back to Tom after the fight on opinion difference that they had over the guy from the bar. This is where they should have ended it. And Summer went to Tom once again to apologize. Although Summer had a clear mindset, her unintentional actions provoked Tom to continue to wait for the her. They both were flawed, but Summer was unintentional wrong and Tom was confused and delusionally wrong.

The film has really done a commendable work on pulling off the viewers on to the side of Tom by convincingly portraying the perspective of Tom only. It's good that the movie had a good warm ending.

While everyone asks for "500 days of Autumn", I want "500 days of Tom" from Summer's perspective.

Name: Rough Cut
Review: This film has a really unique plot. The film is written in such a way that I am still confused of who is the protagonist and the antagonist of this film. The film itself changes its shape by how we perceive it. Both the characters are written so good and has better character arc. Each of their character mirrors one-other's. Like how the actor(soo ta) acts tough but isn't, and the gangster is genuinely tough but acts calm. The film progresses and their relationship evolves in such a way that they are both protagonists of their own narratives. SPOILER SEGMENT AHEAD!!! Actually I was completely confused at the end of the film when Gangster says that he is going to shoot a movie and the actor is the camera for, then he kills Mr. Park right in front of the him. In that moment, I couldn’t tell what was real and what was part of the act. But when the police caught Gangster, the expression on his face made me realize something profound—he was saying that everything that they had done for the film wasn’t real acting. The true performance is the one you put-on in your everyday life. The masks you wear to fit into society—that is the real acting, and in that sense, every human is a great actor. And this is just my thoughts about the end of the film. SPOILER SEGMENT OVER !!! The beauty of the film "Rough Cut" is that it does not provide clear answers. Instead, it leaves the audience in a state of confusion, just like the actor. And we are left with a realization that we too, are acting every day, playing roles in a grand, unscripted film called OUR LIFE.

Name: Hey Ram
Review: I STILL CAN'T BELIVE THAT THIS IS HIS FIRST ORIGINAL DIRECTIONAL VENTURE OF KAMAL HASSAN SIR. Naadi, nerambu, ratham sadhai, buthi ellathulaiyum cinema ooripona orutharaala mattum dhaan indha maari oru padam eduka mudiyum. My Respect for AANDAVAR Day By Day 🛐📈

Name: Hey Ram
Review:
Hey Ram is not just a film—it is a cinematic masterpiece that blends history, fiction, sentiment, redemption, and guilt by delivering an emotionally immersive experience. The film also provides a visually stunning experience through its exceptional cinematography. The use of color plays an important role in storytelling by differentiating the timelines—the flashback is presented in colour sequence , while the present timeline is depicted in black and white.

The performances of all the lead actors are commendable, with Kamal Haasan, Shah Rukh Khan, and Atul Kulkarni delivering exceptionally powerful performances. When it comes to the soundtrack, The Great Ilaiyaraaja’s work is nothing short of incredible. The usage of the interlude of the song "Ram Ram" in the final sequence, where the black-and-white frame gradually transforms into a color sequence still haunts me with the past of the Saket Ram.

Watching the film for the 2nd time in a single day, I noticed something. This is just my own introspection of the film.

SPOILER--AHEAD

In the film’s final act, Saket Ram is feeling guilty for triggering a riot in Chandni Chowk, which leads to the deaths of many people, including innocent children. When the riots end, the survivors see him as their savior, but those who knew he was the cause of the violence in the first place are all dead. He is desperate to confess his sins, but he finds himself trapped—as there is no one left who knows the truth. When he finally decides to confess to Mahatma Gandhi, The history takes it's own course and, Gandhi is assassinated by Godse, leaving Ram with a burden that he can never unload the weight of his Sins. Now he has to live the rest of his life seeking for the forgiveness that he can never attain.

This realization makes his world fall into a darkness, which is visually represented by the black-and-white sequences in the present timeline. In the end, when Ram dies, the present timeline, which was depicted in black and white sequence, shifts to color sequence—symbolizing that in the afterlife, he has finally confessed his sins to those affected by the riots and has sought the forgiveness that was denied to him in life.

Name: The Pianist
Review: This film is brutally heartbreaking and so truthful in portraying the reality of war. Adrien brody has played his role of "Wlady Szpilman" so good that you can never see the actor, and only see the character "Szpilman". There aren't much films where you think that the main lead should rather die that going through this immense pain in his daily life. "The Pianist" is one such film. Spoiler Segment Ahead!!! Here in this film Wlady's suffering is portrayed in a raw realistic tone, he is living with a constant fear of death, hunger, and isolated from the outer world. At one point his life feels less like surviving and more of a slow erosion, where he is neither living nor dying. This is where you start to think that he should rather Die than living this miserable life. Disclaimer: For all those weak hearted people out there, this masterpiece is not for you. ❗NOT ALL FILMS ARE MEANT TO BE FUN❗

Name: Server Sundaram
Review:
I just can't believe that this film is 61 years old. If you want to summarise the entire tamil cinema in 100 film, this is definitely gonna be in that list. This is a timeless classical masterpiece of the tamil cinema industry.

Hats off to the writer and the director for perfectly staging the the scene where Nagesh speaks highly of his server uniform. I have known that KB sir is a great director, but re-watching this film as an adult made me realise how great of a writer he was.

Last but not the least, this film may have the best direction, best writing and whatever, but what stands above all of them is the commendable performance of the legendary actor Nagesh Sir.

Hollywood: We have Charlie Chaplin 💅🏻.
Indian Cinema: We have our Nagesh 😌.

Name: Atonement
Review: The "Atonement' is a kinda film that mesmerises you with its magical cinematography, that great cinematography made me feel like a outsider to the film, making me emotionally distant from the characters. Those people who regularly read my reviews knows that I personally like the films that has good character arc and development. The films paving is too slow, and some people may say that films focusing on characters have slow pacing to get emotionally connected to the viewer, but still this was such a SLOW-BURN. This film majorly focuses on three main characters (Briony, Robbie, Sesilia) in which Briony had the most character development, then Robbie had a good one, but even though sesilia is a main character and film focuses on them, still her character doesn't have much development, and her motives are not clearly portrayed or we people can't understand it. Then coming to Briony, she had the most character development, her way of realising her mistake and living with guilt made the viewers to get attached to her character but what she did in her past makes us anger on her, I was in a dilemma of what I feel about that character. These are the reason why the film felt too emotionally distant to me. Then the ending twist felt like it was forcefully fitted into the film to make it more impactful, which made me feel like it was some kind of emotional manipulation towards the viewer. In short: "Atonement" is a film beautiful film that has great performances from the leads, but Still works only in few parts. Atonement: 3/5⭐

Name: Meiyazhagan
Review: Last night around 11 PM, while scrolling through Spotify for something to fall asleep to, I ran into a podcast about MEIYAZHAGAN. I thought I should play it in the background and fall asleep. But instead of putting me to sleep, it woke me up emotionally, mentally, entirely. As the host talked about the film, scene by scene, I wasn’t just listening, I was visualizing. Every frame. Every conversation. Every silence. Then slowly, I was overwhelmed by nostalgic memories, and it felt soft but hit me hard inside. And I cried. I cried through the entire podcast, not out of sadness, but from the Pure-Wholesomeness and emotional depth it brought back. The podcast was almost the length of the film, but I didn’t want it to end. By the time the podcast ended, I had made up my mind. I had to rewatch MEIYAZHAGAN today and I did it (I watched in piracy cuz the uncut version is not available on OTT). But I won’t say much about the film itself, because everything I ever wanted to say about MEIYAZHAGAN was said in that podcast. My older logs already hold my reviews. This isn't about reviewing the film. It's about giving a shoutout to that podcast. To anyone who thinks they love Meiyazhagan, Please listen to this podcast by "Ice Biriyani". Here is the Podcast link: Spotify

Name: 3BHK
Review: 3bkh is film that feels personal at a lot of scenes. The film's first half was great, and do the second half, but the first half was my favourite. I could relate a lot to siddarth's character, because he was afraid of failure and so do me. I was totally flabbergasted and my eyes were full of tears when siddarth speaks about his failures " I'm afraid of failure, I have lost hope in life for failing a lot, but there is my father, no matter how many times he fails he still stand up tall, I wanna be like him" these words resonated so much into me like I was saying those words. There are a lot of moments like this in the first half for you to relate this with your life. Multiple spoilers Ahead!!! Then comes the second half, it starts with where the first half left, but as the film progresses, there are few scenes which made me feel like unnecessary when first included, but then they gave justifications to them. I wanna mention those scenes "the introduction of the heroine again into the film, i personally felt that this heroine should be left like how the "devathai teacher" from pariyerum perumal was left". But they included the heroine character into the film again, and I was like mehhh, but then as the story progresses siddarth says "you are the only decision that I made with my whole heart, this is very important for siddharth character development and arc". Then another scene is when the sister breaks down, i feared that it would goes as the typical societal norms like adjust and move on, but thank God it didn't go that way. Overall this is a good film that has multiple heartwarming and close to reality scenes that hits you hard. 3bhk : 3.25/5⭐

Name: Cinema Paradiso
Review: Cinema Paradiso starts as a regular film, but as the film progresses it captures every last bit of the essence of life and transforms it into a soulful movie. It's been around 3 years since I first watched this film, I was not born in a generation where theater is the only entertainment, but my father is from that generation and he has a lot of memories. I used to listen to every stories my father tells about his experiences of going to a movie theater, from making a fake sleeping human using pillows at night and going to night shows at theater which is 15km away from his village, He and his friends drive cycle for 15km and go watch the movie and returns home before 4am, to avoid getting caught by my grandma. He also remembers and used to say how much was the tickets priced for the film he went, how drastically the prices increased over time. I will be totally invested into his stories, not because they are entertaining, but because they are his precious memories, part of his youth days which is full of life. The most beautiful part of listening to the memories is that, it feels like you are time travelling through their eyes. The reason I shared all these is because cinema paradise didn't just celebrated cinema, it felt like watching my dad's childhood memories on screen. There is something very magical about the films that makes you feel NOSTALGIC for moments you have never lived. And That's PEAK CINEMA ❤️‍🩹. Cinema Paradiso is PEAK CINEMA ❤️‍🩹 🛐 Cinema Paradiso: 10/5⭐ The Five Stars aren't Enough!!!

Name: DNA

The film is better when you trim the intro of lead, and his flashback sequence, those are totally useless for the main plot. Although I could say a lot negatives, the film kept me engaged. Last But not Least "Ennada paatu idhella!? Nallave illa 🤧" DNA: 3.25/5⭐

Name: Maargan

Name: Johnny
Review: THALAIVAR looks so freaking great in Jhonny. This is a totally worth rewatch. I can't ever talk of this film without speaking of Raja - The GOAT. The soundtrack of this film is soothing, mesmerizing, addictive, looping, and what so on. I can't get out of the soundtrack in my mind. I just wanna insert the "Three Dragon template" for RAJINI-MAGENDIRAN-RAJINI. 🐉-🐉-🐉 But The One and Only ISAIGNANI ILAYARAJA 🛐🛐🛐 being the fourth dragon (The mother of the all three above). JHONNY: 5/5⭐

Name: Incendies
Review: This is my rewatch and I still remember the day if went "aaaahhhhhhwwwww_____😶😶‍🌫️" when that banger twist dropped at me for the first time (I was not shocked to that level in recent years). I know this is a slow watch, but the plot twist and the experience you experience is unmatched. That was totally a "Expect the Unexpected" moment TBH. I have nothing more to say, but remember 1+1=1 Incendies: 5/5⭐

Name: Mahavatar Narsimha
Review: Am I the only one who thinks that this film would have been even more better if it was made as a 2D animated film!? And the background scores by Sam CS is amazing, but not all songs in this film had me. But the dubbing team did a terrific work-Hats Off!!! Mahavatar Narasimha: 3.5/5⭐

Name: Devara: Part 1
Review: If this review is about the film, then it is okayish but if it's about how I enjoyed this film then this is a better film. While I was at the gym today morning, someone randomly played the title track of devara, and that track's dope hit me to the core. Actually this is just a regular masala film, but when you experience this film by enjoying Anirudh's Music, the film gets to a whole another level. In the first watch this film wasn't as good as this, cause I wasn't concentrating much on Ani's music, But this rewatch is totally different. The whole Cast and Crew should thank Ani for elevating their work. Devara Part 1: 3.5/5⭐

Name: Parandhu Po
Review: Parandhu Po is a light hearted breezy film from Ram. Mirchi Siva and that Kid is the man of the hour, they just captivate you into their life. And more importantly the director wanted to refill you all the hopes of your life, that there is not even a single negative character in the film, "ellarum aniyayathuku nallavangala irukanga yaa". Enna dhaan ipdi la sonnalum padam paaka start aagi oru 15 mins dhaan, you forget that you are watching a film, and just see this like a bunch of clips are playing on the screen and it's very nice and pleasingly joyful to watch those clips. For those people who ask - "Ipdi la panna... Oru Rendu Adi pota sari aagiduvan..." - Shiva doesn't hit his kid not because he don't hit him, but because he doesn't wanna hit that kid!!! This is what makes him the person who he really is!!! PS: I have a clear thought of what I wanna convey in the last para about dad's character, but I can't find the appropriate words to explain them properly, so if anyone could understand what I wanna say, please do convey it by using right choice of words in the comments ✌🏽. Paranthu Po: 3.75/5 ⭐

Name: 8 Thottakkal
Review: A well made film that has great writing. MS Bhaskar and Vetri are the two pillars of this film. 8 thottakkal: 4/5⭐

Name: Kung Fu Hustle

Name: Villadhi Villain

Name: Ranga

Name: Coolie
Review:
Coolie had a great potential to be, from the way it is launched towards in the banger interval, but the latter half of the film kills the entire potential of the film with lazy writing and over-dependance on the so called Theater moment (Cameos). Talking about the cameos, Aamir Khan and Upendra adds nothing, but the lenght of the film by requiring slow-motion shots focusing their characters. The only positive in watching this film is the theatre experience.

Coolie shatters all the expectations and becomes nothing but the worst written venture of Lokesh Kanagaraj. 

Coolie: 2.5/5⭐

Name: Friends
Review: Thanks ra Sun TV, romba naal ku aprm nalla sirichu oru padam pathen !! As I said earlier, 90s and early 2k Tamil cinema has the highest repeat value for sure. No more arguments!! Contractor Nesamani VaAzhga 🛐🙏🏽 Friends: 3.5/5⭐

Name: Kushi
Review: Although it's been 25 years, this film hasn't aged. Iniku paakurapo kuda padam nalla fresh ah super ah iruku. The soundtrack of this film is just like Fine Wine, time aaga aaga innum super ah maatite podhu!!! Kushi: 4/5⭐

Name: Sitaare Zameen Par
Review: This film is an example of how to be true to its original work and yet perfectly blend the Indian style of work in the film. Aamir Khan's performance is so good as usual. Sitaare zameen par: 3.75/5⭐

Name: Mission: Impossible – The Final Reckoning
Review: I have seen mission impossible films driven by action, romance,drama and what all... But this Finale film is driven by Guilt, establishing the character traits in a strongest way, concluding this Great Saga !! MI-Final Reckoning: 4/5⭐

Name: C/o Kancharapalem
Review: Saying anything other than an one word review "Definitely worth" would end up being a spoiler. One of the best slice of life film. Ending my review right here!!! Do give this one a shot and you will never regret it!!! C/O Kancherapaalem: 4.5/5⭐

Name: Maareesan
Review: Maareesan had a great first half ending with a banger interval, but from then the story takes a turn and still manages to keeps its soul alive. But as soon as they start to explain the reasons behind the actions of Mareesan with a "naangalum karuthu solla porom" kinda flashback the movie falls down from a "could have been great" to a mid teir film. Apart from the story, FaFa and Vaigaipuyal Vadivelu are the two pillars that holds the film. Vaigaipuyal literally said "hold my beer" to people who say he is just a comedian. PS: I really loved the "Nethu Oruthara Oruthara" song sequence a lot !! Maareesan : 2.5/5⭐

Name: The Princess Bride
Review: Isn't the kid being a hypocrite when he interrupted for "the kissing scene" and doesn't stop when he heard "Son of a Bitch" !!! The Princess Bride: 3.25/5⭐

Name: Sethu...
Review: I have already seen this film in bits and pieces, but this is my first full watch. This is undoubtedly a Gem of Tamil cinema. When a filmmaker is cen-percent sure of what he is gonna deliver, this is how the film turns to be. Although the ending is truly hard hitting, it didn't affect me much cuz I have already known/seen it in pieces. Thanks to this film for introducing/reintroducing two legends of Tamil Cinema Bala Sir and Chiyaan Vikram 🛐🛐 Sethu: 3.75/5⭐

Name: Kingdom
Review: A Good script on paper that is wasted on screen. "Kingdom" had a great premises to revolve around, but falls off the cliff with its weak execution. Ani's great soundtrack saved it a bit from turning into a trash. Background voice - The officer says "you said you would burn down everything right?... Go ahead!!!" 💥 The scene cuts and Devarakonda starts trimming his hair 😭😭😭 (WTH MAN) 😭😭😭 The TV screen is flat and so are the emotions in the film 🤧. sorry guys... can't even try to align with the character's motive!!! Last but not least, I beg you guys to make a standalone films and stop with this fucking sequel bullshits 🙏🏽🙏🏽🙏🏽 Kingdom: 2/5⭐

Name: Irudhi Pakkam
Review:
A slightly toned indianised tamil adaptation (inspired) of "Basic Instinct". This is a crime investigation thriller that keeps you engaged. This could have been a better film if it was a little crispy and shorter. Either way a decent watch!!!
Society has made us believe that Virginity for men is in their hearts and women is in their bodies. This is a strong statement!!!
Irudhi Pakkam: 3.25/5⭐

Name: Time
Review:
I think this served as the source material for "The Substance". Although both of them revolves around beauty industry, Substance is more of a fictional world, and Time is close to reality. Time concerntrates more on the psychological aspects of the beauty standards and the sense of craving attention from their partner. There are film they talk a lot to convey less, and there is other type where they talk less to convey a lot. This is of the 2nd type.

This film makes me feel astonished by the way they captured the emotions, mental states of the characters without even trying to do so.

To be honest, the film left me in a void, and i don't have anything to say Right Now!!!

Time: 3.25/5⭐

Name: Naan Kadavul
Review:
Naan Kadavul is a rare film that discusses the ideology of God and still be liked by my theist dad and his Atheist son (myself)!!!

Director Bala Sir made a Raw and grounded film that explores Cruelty in its most raw form, and the Karma Principles. Although the film has one of the best comedy scenes in the surface level, this is one of the most intriguing hard drama that is very painful on the deeper introspection of the film. Ilaiyaraja sir with his music had tied ourselves into the world of the film and ARYA's performance made can't see "ARYA as ARYA anymore"!!!

Naan Kadavul: 4.5/5⭐

Name: F1
Review:
POV A Sunday morning, You shut your room-turn on the Screens-put on your headphones-plays F1 The movie- and getting totally imerrsed into the world of F1 is pure CINEMATIC HIGH!!

Forever thankful to the makers of this film for making such a heartwarming film. I have already made a statement... And I'm gonna repeat myself again!!!
Iranian films are the simplistic representation of the complexities of the human life. PERIOD.

Name: 7G Rainbow Colony
Review:
You can even judge me for loving this film, and I would still love it to the core !!
7G எனும் காவியம் இயற்றிய செல்வராகவன் எனும் மகாகவி-க்கு எனது கோடான கோடி நன்றிகள் 🙏🏼🙏🏼🛐🛐
I have watched this film so many times on television and they always trim the film for its duration and sexual content. This is my first time I watched the whole 185mins film. I have always liked this film, but from now on, i would say I love this film. Everything in this film is so good, from its story, direction, acting, soundtrack, lyrics, and the character arc. The most valid argument around this film on internet is that the main lead is a stalker, and does wierd things. I don't wanna justify his actions, But his Character Arc does it. He does all those wrong doings without knowing its wrong, and all those things defines him as a person and that's who he is. You should always know that the albums is gonna be a Goated Album when Na MuthuKumar - Selvaragavan - Yuvan are working on it. 7G Rainbow Colony: 5/5⭐

Name: The Queen's Gambit

Name: Mozhi
Review: Mozhi is a wholesome lighthearted film, and it taught me how to say "Sorry" and "Thank You" in Sign language!! Watching Prakash Raj as Viji is so on another level of laughter watch. Mozhi: 3.5/5⭐

Name: Uttama Villain
Review:
I really needed this rewatch. KamalHassan the writer is so Underrated in the over-the-top shadow of the legendary actor KamalHassan. We always praise Aandavar for his acting prowess, leaving the great writer inside him unnoticed. The film is filled with a lot of enjoyable, captivating, emotional phases that shines with its brilliance in writing. The casting of this film is so Goated right down to the last character played in this film - starting from his Real life Guru KB sir (as margadarisi - guru of mahoranjan) and K Vishwanath sir and Ms Bhaskar, Urvashi, and and ofcourse KamalHassan Himself. As someone who has grown up watching countless classics of Tamil cinema and fed on the bts of those classics, this film is a poem that hits you with pure nostalgia till its last pulse. Uttama villain isn't just a Film - it is KamalHassan putting his soul into writing, philosophy and mortality of life and immortality of Art and the legacy of cinema.
சாகா வரம் போல் சோகம் உண்டோ!!!
ஆம்... எண்ணிக்கையில்லா சாதனை செய்து... சாதல் இல்லா சாபம் வாங்கிய கலைஞன் நீ !!! Uttama Villain - 5/5⭐

Name: Madharaasi
Review: SK took the "Gun uhm endhu dhaan... Ponnu uhm endhu dhaan" too seriously!! Madharasi becomes one of great action feast that keeps you hooked with its adrenaline pumping action sequences and melodramatic soft touch of emotions. A great film doesn't mean it don't have any logical loopholes or anything, and Cinema isn't about making a perfectly logical script. Cinema is "24 lies per second" and a great film means how convincingly they have made the audience believe their lies. In that perspective, Madharasi is a great film. Not that I wanna stand out alone, but I didn't really like this soundtrack except for few moments and it's the negative that got me off the hook sometimes. Madharasi: 3.75/5⭐

Name: Love, Rosie
Review:

Love, Rosie: 2.75/5⭐

Name: Varisu
Review: Maybe it was about my mindset that I liked this film on the first watch, and on the rewatches it keeps getting worst than ever. The Humour irritated me a lot on this watch. Varisu : 1.5/5⭐

Name: Virumaandi
Review: I don't think I'm qualified enough to review this film, so let me just share my experience of watching this film. That was around 2020, I was just starting to explore to world cinema (I'm still exploring), I used to watch the top rated movies of imdb at random, on one fine day I chose to play this film, and watched this fully with my family and it left me mesmerized with the rashomon effect (My first time experiencing the Rashomon effect). The film follows a certain pattern when Kothalathevan narrates the story, and Virumaandi is the bad guy in that POV, When his POV ends and we come back to the present timeline we see kamal in a negative way, his comments on Rohini makes them even stronger by not letting his protagonist side on top.(AND TODAY, I just triggered my female friend by not trying to justify his comment on Rohini but still ended up giving reasons to his actions and triggering my friend even more... I know it sounds confusing, but it is what it is!!!). But when the story unfolds from Virumaandi's perspective, everything is totally different and the roles are reversed now. This transition made me think "What the Fuck man, this is absolutely amazing" and "Gommala Twist uhh!!!". After completing the film, I have had one hell of a rollercoaster of emotions throughout the film. I was just wandering amazed by this film for straight 2 days and I would never forget that. Forever Thanking you AANDAVAR for messing up my mind with this masterpiece ‼️ Virumaandi : 5/5⭐

"Hi Malini 👋🏻, I'm mathew, naan idha unkita solliye aaganum, nee avlo Azhagu 🏵️, Inga yaarum ivlo azhaga... Oru azhaga paathuruka maatanga✨, and I'm in love with you 💗 !!!" Plays Mundhinam Paarthene 💌

Name: The Big Short
Review: People tend to get on my nerves when they call this FUCKING GAMBLING, Hey youu... This isn't FUCKING GAMBLING, this is an indigenous piece of mindful calculation and foresight. Give the respect that this move deserves and don't you dare compare it to absurd Gambling. This isn't some common man's cup of tea, as the whole plot and the concepts discussed here are way too dense and confusing, but the sharp screenplay with those clever 3rd wall breaking makes all those complexities understandable and engaging to watch. Overall The Big Short is an engaging financial drama that ends up as a great watch. PS: A Shoutout to The OG Vijay VaradhaRaj and his "Finance Series" on Meta Monkeys youtube channel. If not for him, I wouldn't have understood these money concepts so well. The Big Short: 4.5/5⭐

Name: Salaar: Part 1 - Ceasefire
Review: Salaar is a film that gets better with rewatch. I think this must be my 6th or 7th time watching it. What makes this so rewatchable is Prashanth Neel’s fantastic world-building, the way he stages each scene, and the non-linear storytelling that ties it all together. This movie has an enormous runtime of 175 minutes, yet the non-linear staging makes it stand apart. Prashanth Neel stages this like stretching a rubber band by pulling it further and further, building tension without release. Just when the audience says “Enough already, let it snap” he finally lets go of it. And when that release comes, the sequence lands perfectly on target, elevating the scene to an entirely different level. Well, coming to Salaar, the rubber band was stretched a bit too long that the entire first half felt like it was just pulling the string. But when Neel begins unfolding the strings one by one in the final act, the long stretching slowly fades with each drop, and all that waiting pays off in full when the final blow lands. But please next part la andha dialogues mattum konjam regular pace la pesuna panna nalla irukum, aniyayathuku porumaiya pesuringa da yebbah 🙏🏼 Salaar Part 1 Ceasefire: 4/5⭐

Name: Velaiyilla Pattathari
Review: I never knew until now that this was Dhanush's 25th film. But I still remember how much the "Young Me" was excited while watching this on the first day of release. This film will always hold a special place in my heart, cuz this is the first film that made me cry during the watch. I never expected that I would ever cry while watching a movie and this proved me wrong!! this taught me that expressing all your emotions watching a movie is not a crime and it's one of the best ways to restore your mental peace. And there is the "Current Me" who loves expressing emotions during the watch. Thank you! "VIP" for teaching me one of the greatest lessons in my life. VIP is an absolutely entertaining film that blends all the emotions, nuance comedy, and mass elements that are taken to a whole another league with Anirudh's Background score. This has a high rewatch calibre and stands tall even on a hundredth rewatch. Peak Anirudh - Peak Dhanush - Peak Cinema 🛐 - 🛐 - 🛐 VIP: 5/5⭐ ( I love This to the Core)

Name: Vetri Kodi Kattu
Review: The 90s and the early 2k are the golden age of re-watch films!! Period!! A film that follows the typical Rags to Riches template that has been beaten to the core for multiple times, but still the film manages to engage the viewers with its powerfully written emotions that drives the plot with its solid screenplay. Asusual Vaigai puyal scores a maximum with his extremely unique body language and his humour sense. The Track Comedy from the 90s era has its own fanbase. And the combo of Parthiban-Vadivrlu is on their own league on the top teir. Vetri kodi kattu: 3.5/5⭐

Name: Kiss
Review: A Fantasy Padam in tamil cinema is a "Karanam thappuna Maranam" situation and Kiss is neither on the cliff, nor falling down. Rather it's hung on the rope (Humour) that is tied to the cliff. I loved the Humour sense of Sathish Krishnan in his interviews for this film and decided that I should watch this film. Now it's on OTT i gave it a shot and it has really good humour scenes. Oru Jolly aana padam paatha feel ✨. A kind request to the tamil directors to take more of Jolly aana padam. Kiss: 3/5⭐ (½ for the jolly moments)

Name: Kaantha
Review: Kaantha is a film that stays a contemplorary masterpiece in the first half and then the generic second half dips from the masterpiece to a great film and then there comes the ending that spoils the whole rhythm of the film. It's just don't even remember when almost every actors compete each other in a film. From Bhagya shree to Samuthirakani. Ivanga rendu peru dhaan potti potu nadikuranga nu paatha DQ la vera maari. I have seen organic acting, but here DQ has extracted every last bit of acting from his every organs ( Oruthar organic acting panni paathuruken... Aana orutharoda ellam organs um act panni paathadhu illa). I have heard somewhere that "concious overacting" is the most toughest part, and I could clearly see that in this film. Annan (DQ) ku oru National Award parcel !! I wanna say a lot but I don't have the words so let me simply list it- Things I loved - constant shift between the retro theme and the contemporary visuals - Peak performances from all the lead actors- Ego Clash between TMK AND Ayya Good but kinda toned down - the good genre shift in the second half but something was missing which made the first half so fucking Great. The Let down - The ending Kaantha : 3.75/5⭐

Review: An absolute family entertainer supported by emotions and seamlessly placed humour that takes the watching experience to enjoyable watch. Brahmanandam Sir, you are truly a legend sir. Humour is highly personal, and I enjoyed this a lot. Brindavanam: 3.5/5⭐

Name: Bison Kaalamaadan
Review: Bison is a kinda film that pull you into its world and makes you root for the characters. The most beautiful part is how it portrayed the two deuteroganist (Ameer and Lal) they both are against and fight each other, but they are both morally right from their perspective. I loved the way this film showed how the people tend to forget the motive of the revolt and turn the revolt into a caste based Kalavaram. We can't blame anyone for this, but the transition for the motive is a canon event and very natural. I wasn't expecting MariSelvaraj to make a well balanced film without taking a stand but still manages to transit the right stand. Coming to the Actors, Pasupathy sir summa pichi otharitinga sir 🛐 dhruv's genetics has started to kick off. A terrifically steller show from Dhruv and Pasupathy sir. Is it only me or anybody else out there who thinks "Mari makes a better film, when he takes a neutral stand!" !? Well on the whole, Bison turns out to be a great watch. Bison: 4.25/5 ⭐

Name: Grave of the Fireflies
Review: How is something which is this beautiful be this much painful!? Rewatching this again when I wanna feel pain! Spoiler Ahead!!! There isn't much film that makes you feel that they should rather die than living with pain. Atleast they would be peaceful in their afterlife. Grave of Fireflies is one of them. Although I wanted them to get rid of this pain, I can't bear that they died this way.

I loved the irony of how the film that talks  "family caste" is ideologically against the caste and caste based violence. Thevar Magan will be a timeless classic of the Tamil Cinema.

Name: Twinkling Watermelon
Review: I’m still not sure why I felt the urge to rewatch this, but I'm glad I did. I really needed this rewatch. It turned out to be such a heartwarming experience. It’s been a couple of days since I started rewatching this. I’m usually not the kind of person who watches things slowly, I’m more of a “once I start, I have to finish it as soon as possible” type. But this time, I couldn’t do that. Maybe it’s because I’m in the middle of my semester exams, or maybe because it was a rewatch… or maybe because, somewhere deep down, I wanted to feel it like a slow poison flowing gently into my veins. Whatever the reason, it felt perfect. I loved every bit of it. Heyyy @Shahaana, thank you so much for recommending this drama in the first place. I might have watched it a little too late (okay, maybe a few months), but I still loved it with my whole heart on the first watch and even more now. Once again, thank you!! Viva La Vida ✨

Name: Kuruthipunal
Review: Kuruthipunal is hard hitting masterpiece that stays true to its soul. Blindfolded Ideology vs moral righteousness !! This isn't not a regular Cop vs Terrorist film. This is RAW-GRITTY-STYLISH AND UNAPOLOGETIC. Kuruthipunal not just stops with placing two men with opposing ideologies, but goes deeper exposing their fear, obsession, conflict, and belief. The psychological layers that KamalHasaan and Nassar brings to their character Adhi Narayanan and Badri cannot be acted, they lived it!! The Screenplay, Characterization and Plot alone is enough to elevate this film to a cult classic status. And Then, there comes PC Sriram... Every frame, every fucking frame dipped in style and aesthetic visuals that never Soften the rawness, rather amplify it's raw beauty. Ohh Man, the dialogues were so freaking Sharp and right on point. When you see dialogue - Written by KamalHasaan, you must already know that this is gonna be a straight shot on head, hard hitting and stays longer and age like a fine wine. I wanna quote my favourite lines, but I'd end up quoting the entire film 😭. NO Matter how hard I try not to OverRate KamalHasaan, This man proves me wrong and I Fail miserably, Every Single Time!! Because this man, just keeps on evolving, and so his love and passion towards his Art. My Respect Towards him keeps on Peaking 📈📈🛐🛐

Name: Meiyazhagan
Review:
Yesterday was one of the worst days of my life that I would never forget. I was really craving for some comfort.

Meiyazhagan isn't just a Film, Meiyazhagan is a Healer ❤️‍🩹. No matter what my emotions are, be it Sadness, lonely, Happy, Joy, Emptyness and anything else, Meiyazhagan will always remain the Shelter that I turn back whenever I want Lift Up my mood. May all of you guys feel the peace this film brings.

This rewatch felt like one big warm hug, but this time I need some more to heal myself. If Meiyazhagan isn't enough, there is only One Man who could heal me completely. So here I'm turning towards IlaiyaRaaja to heal myself.

I hope I'll be better in few days.

Name: Sarpatta Parambarai
Review:
Sarpatta Parambarai is a well written and masterfully crafted motivational film with a typical underdog story but still manages to stay unique and distinctive with it's raw touch and realistic characters. This doesn't just tell the story of Rags to Riches, instead it establishes a world set in 1970s Chennai and multiple characters and their journey. Underdog - Rise to Peak - Crash to humiliation - Comeback.
"Parambarai la innathuku da manatha eduthunu vandhu veikringa!!"
For me this line is one of the most pewerful and subtle political dialogue in the last decade of Tamil Cinema!! I was really craving for some "lift me up" and this was absolutely right in point. Thanks Pa Ranjith na for this.
✋🏻 Wammala Idhaan Da Motivation ✋🏻📈📈

Name: La La Land
Review:
Oh man, what an absolutely beautiful film this is. Every single frame feels like a painting, so pleasing to the eyes that it’s hard to look away. I honestly can’t admire any cinematography more than this.

When I first watched it, I didn’t know it was a musical, and musicals aren’t My Cup of Tea. Yet somehow, this film still managed to impress me.

Recently I was scrolling thru shorts on YouTube and saw an Edit of La La Land, then decided to rewatch this. 

On this re-watch, I started to notice how beautiful is this film. I accept that the soundtrack of this film is great, but in my mind, Tamil songs by Ilaiyaraaja and A. R. Rahman kept playing instead. I wasn’t actually listening to them. They just came naturally as I watched the film. My eyes were watching the visuals, while my mind plays a different music in the background. It felt like I was watching a completely new movie, but with the emotions and connections being elevated to the top. 

It was a different experience but I loved this.

Review: Partially enjoyable moments kept too far away with extremely dull scenes in between. Is my attention span really bad or the film is not engaging!? Yaaravadhu sollunga bha 🙏🏻 Revolver Rita: 2.25/5⭐

Name: Meiyazhagan
Review:
Meiyazhagan suthama pudikadhavanga kuda sendhu pathen, and they said "Chi... Ithellam oru padam ah da... Summa serial maari pesine irukanga". This watch was on vijay super and we discussed about the film on every break. I explained them why this film is so good, and the arguments heated up. 

So I thought there is no more point in arguing, and let's just agree with their opinion on this film. Sometimes acceptance is far better than winning an argument. 

No matter what others say about this film, this will always remain close to my heart.

Name: Middle Class
Review: Middle Class is a film that tries hard to be rooted and natural but ends up feeling artificial at places. It had a good potential and better on papers but then fall short with its execution and forcefully fit character arcs. How come someone who was never into communism in the first place suddenly becomes a communist with a 5 mins sitting on a park and a single dream. That was totally disappointing and felt forcefully fed to audience. If you really wanna feed communism, then atleast try giving them proper leads with that particular chracter so that the transformation doesn't feel like dumping. Other than that this was an Okayish padam. Middle Class: 2.75/5⭐

Name: Paint Drying
Review:
Movies are no longer what they used to be for me.

At a point of time in my life, they were my teachers, companion, doctor and my therapist. They helped me understand emotions that I even couldn’t describe. They offered comfort when I didn’t know where else to turn.

But now, they feel like nothing more than moments of distraction with temporary escapes that pull me away from real world and my unresolved inner conflicts.

I think I lost myself somewhere down the line in search of a place better than reality.

I’ve come to realize that inner conflicts must be confronted in the moment they begin. Escaping into distractions only delays the inevitable. All the comfort that is found elsewhere is temporary distractions from the problem, but the real problem resides deep within you.

Ippo illanaalum, namma future la kandipa adha face panni dhaan aaganum.

So instead of running from reality, No matter how uncomfortable and difficult it may be, It’s better to face what’s within us. because that’s the only path to real healing.

It's not that I gonna stop watching movies from now on, It's about me not gonna use the movies as curtains to hide behind anymore.

A re-watch can't get any better than this.

Name: Maaveeran
Review: Maaveeran is that one film that I keep on enjoying more on a rewatch than the last watch. Cuz I tend to notice all those little details that makes us believe in the fantasy subject and the world of Maaveeran. I enjoyed Maaveeran !! I liked Maaveeran !! I love Maaveeran !!

Name: Azhagiya Tamil Magan
