Name: Kotaro Lives Alone
Review:
It started as a great funny and light-hearted anime and as the time passes it reveals it's true self and makes us feel disturbed and sorrow.

It's depressing as much as it is heartwarming.

Name: Thenali
Review:
Thenali is funny - quirky - chaotic - dramatic and criminally underrated imo and as much as I want it to be out of the radar, I want people to enjoy it and appreciate it.

Thenali feels like nothing but a breath of a fresh air which makes you calm slow and begin to agree that not all troubles are always big and its about how you handle it (atleast it made me feel like one).

What I wanna say is - you guys are all stressed up and why don't you give this a try and relax with Thenali.

Name: Sirai
Review: A simple Cop drama story with a nearly packed sequences that talks religious politics in a simple way but leaves a large impact. The bgm and story sets the tone as a thriller at first and then takes a tonal shift that you would never expect by the halfway of the film. Cops are not super-beings, they are humans too. Overall Sirai is a Solid watch. BTW what is the result of the RDO inquiry of the three cops!? (Yaarukachum therinja sollunga) Sirai: 3.5/5

Name: Thiruvilaiyaadal Aarambam
Review:
American Mappilai and the Manamedai (மணமேடை) is a never ending sad story of Tamil cinema.

Yaaru petha pullaiyo therila ella padathulaiyum heroine ah kalayanam pannikuren nu vandhu emandhutu poran. Enake unna nenacha paavama iruku 😭😂

Name: Akhanda
Review: Boyapati Srinu being too horny for Balayya that he literally made him god. Jai Balayya 🛐🛐 Both are not same 📈📈

Name: Vaa Vaathiyaar
Review: Karthi blasts with MGR (Unintentionally Funny 😭🙏🏻) mannerisms but the film terribly misfires as a whole. I always liked Nalan's taste in music, but this time even the songs weren't good (I just didn't like it).

Name: Dhurandhar
Review: Dhurandhar starts quiet good and then takes its own time to get into the core plot (which almost last over 30 mins). Once it gets into the underworld, the plot doesn't pick it's pace, rather stays low and keeps on building the stakes. The enormous runtime of 3½ hours is justified when all the stakes built up until then, paysoff with a banger final chapter. That one final chapter is so fucking great 📈📈.

Name: Oosaravelli
Review: An high octane action film with a mindlessly funny humour and over the top masala sprinkled all over the film but still ended up between being mediocre and Okayish. Oosaravelli: 2.25/5 ⭐

Name: Andhadhun
Review:
The film was so freaking entertaining that I didn't even realise that "I was hooked into it" until the film ended. 

I always loved the "left to audience" ending, but this time I was like "ohh Man, whyyy 😭😭 Not this time please 😭‼️"

Name: The Invisible Guest
Review:
This was just two people talking the entire duration and I was still hooked to it.

I watched it once a few years back, I might not remember the entire thing, but I remembered the final twist midway the film so It wasn't as impactful as it was in the first watch.

Name: Magadheera
Review:
Someone ~ What's your most favourite film!?
Childhood Me ~ Magadheera (Without hesitation) 

This movie hits with pure Nostalgia ✨.  I was just a kid probably started school (I might not even understand the dialogues fully) but the film just mesmerized me with its absolutely beautiful periodic sequences (undoubtedly the 100 men Fight sequence). I can't say how many time I would have watched that scene and this entire film in my childhood. 

It has been few years since I rewatched this, I know that the uncut version released recently so I thought I should give this a rewatch and here I am remembering my happy childhood days of enjoying this film. No matter at what age I watch this, I would always love this for the happy and mesmerizing memories of this film.

Name: The Rajasaab
Review:
I feel so happy that I didn't waste a penny on this shitty film by going to theater. This was a terrible watch even on OTT.

Rebel saab ku Humour set aagalaya, nadika varalaya, ennane purila. He was literally out of sync in every frame. Absolutely doesn't suit him. But Malavika Mohanan was looking good though 🌝🏃🏼‍♂️‍➡️

Name: Parasakthi
Review:
Parasakthi is a highly relatable story from the heart for every Tamizhan, because we understand the core of what the film is trying to say. It is packed with great performances, powerful and impactful dialogues, and writings with strong payoffs and still the film feels emotionally distant. I could understand the character's pain, but I couldn’t truly feel it. Their sadness didn’t hurt, and their happiness doesn't too. GVP did a great job for the background score and totally let us down with the songs. The film hits the right spot only at a few instances especially just before the interval and during the climax while most of the other scenes fail to land with the same intensity. There are moments where the film suddenly rises to another level, purely because of how well the dialogues are written.
“We need unity and you want uniformity” Absolute peak 🛐
The climax dialogue gave me goosebumps, not because I felt emotionally connected to the film, but because I'm a தமிழ் பேசும் தமிழன். எம் மொழியும் வாழ்க!! உம் மொழியும் வாழ்க!! வாழ்க தமிழ்!! வளர்க தமிழ்!!

Name: Saguni
Review: Apart from the iconic comedy track, there is nothing worth mentioning. A typical Okayish comedy padam from the mid 2k10s.

Name: Pottu Amman
Review: The film that the childhood me liked and the current me laughs at for my taste back then.

Name: Madras
Review: The one film that I hated back then - when I was a kid(about 8 year) because this film doesn't seem like other commercial film and i couldn't understand what and why happens, but now this turns out to be one of the great films that I admire and look up cuz I'm now older enough to understand the voice it hold and it's politics.

Name: Thalaivar Thambi Thalaimaiyil
Review: TTT has a fresh concept that is chaotic to its core and works better when watched with its own audience. For me it was on and off at most of the moments, but when it strikes, it hits right on spot with a banger. I know watching in OTT is my fault, and that too, watching this alone isn't the best decision. This was a good watch, but could have been a lot better and more enjoyable if I watched this together with my friends and family. TTT: 3.25/5⭐

Name: Pichaikkaran 2
Review:
Ewww Brother Ewww!! What's that!?
What's that Brother!? Ewww !!

Name: Meyaadha Maan
Review:
Meyaadha Maan - A minimalistic breezy film that gives an extravagant comfort at heart.
1. ~ Podhuva neenga jalatha Vaai vechi dhaan kudipingala!? ~ En neenga edha vechi kudipinga 🏃🏼‍♂️‍➡️ 2. ~ adipattadhu naduvula, kaal la enda kattu poturukanga 🙄⁉️ Yebbah ultimate gubeer moments ra 🤣😭🤣 (totally unexpected moments but most impactful!!)

Name: Punnagai Desam
Review: There were those days where many breezy light drama films used to release consecutively, but now (2020s) Tamil cinema is really deprived of those kinda movies. We need more movies like this. These movies have high rewatch value for realll!! PS: The Curse of American Mappillai in Tamil cinema never disappoints us!! Punnagai Desam: 3.25/5⭐

Name: Aai
Review: Apart from Vadivelu comedy scenes, the film is barely watchable.

Name: Govindudu Andarivaadele
Review: Vijay Super's Cringe festival !!

Name: Thee
Review:
Absolute Bullshit 💩

Watching two crappy film on the same day was fine until the 2nd film is this one. Padam ah da idhu karumam enna ezhavu neyy therila 💩💩

Name: Sye
Review:
Rajamouli had his own list of crappy film in his filmography from the Pre Magadheera era and they are better left unwatched.
Ohh Man, What's with the misogyny here!?
I usually ignore the misogynistic and sexist commentry in film from back then, until the film does its job to keep me entertained and engaged. This time I deliberately tried to ignore the misogyny in the film, and I failed cause there was nothing much in the film to even make me ignore the misogyny.

If not for the Final act (sports segment), this would be a pure misogynistic film.

Name: The Battle Between Chefs
Review: A mediocre film that works in parts for its humour.

Name: Naari Naari Naduma Murari
Review: The film might land right with back to back Humour but loses its grip in emotional storytelling, thus feeling awkwardly distant to connect with the characters psychology. Naari Naari Naduma Murari: 2.25/5 ⭐

Name: The Machinist
Review: Christian Bale, Are you a human? How are you still alive after all These!? Am man being lean if not tough, but a regualr looking man transforming to a lean guy is tough, that too for this level - this would be as brutal as hell. There are a lot of films where we say, he didn't act he lived the role. They might be some exaggeration. Here that's not even a case, Christian Bale literally survived hell being this character!! Hats off to You !!

Name: Limitless
Review: They say that this is a sci-fi film, and all that I could see is - a guy cooking his wild fantasies into the script and made a film about it. Limitless: 3.25/5⭐

Name: Meiyazhagan
Review: Entertainment kaaga illa... Enlightenment kaaga ✨

Name: Ko
Review: It's been almost 15 years since the film released. I was studying 1st during that time, The only thing that I liked about this film back then was the Aga Naga song and bike stunts sequences with jiiva. But rewatching now makes me see all the details that have went into the writing of the film. I have heard multiple people say things about the ending of this film, That was totally new in that period and people just celebrated the twist in the end. Later on KV Anand made his trademark by keeping a banger twist in all his folling works. This is just a commercial film that is engaing, entertaining and well made in all perspective. It just kills me to see how much the quality of the commercial cinema in Kollywood has degraded from peak Commercial cinema in 2010s to a lazy written slow-motions filled with Cameos in recent days. We need this type of writing to go into the commercial film to revive the good old days of Kollywood. Ko: 4.25/5 ⭐

Name: Lady Vengeance
Review: As expected from Park Chan Wook, he had his touch in the final of the Vengeance Trilogy. Although the Revenge is not as disturbing as in Oldboy, this film was disturbing in its own sense (Not to mention the emotional tension between the mom the daughter). The actress who played the lead role literally slayed in acting. She didn't speak a lot with word, rather a lot with eyes. Was the Film good: Yes !! Will I re-watch it: Probably No !! Lady Vengeance: 3.75 ~ 4 /5⭐

Name: Cook Up a Storm
Review: The best part of watching a film on cooking competition is to watch the chef's cooking skills. They are just so much satisfying to watch. But here,the rest of the film doesn't work properly. Cook Up a Storm: 2.75/5 ⭐

Name: Kung Fu Panda
Review: Nothing can beat the good old days of watching and enjoying this film on Chutti TV. Even rewatching those films feels like drinking a nectar of Nostalgiaaa. Not to mention the stylish animation and the banger Score from Hans Zimmer. That's peak Cinema 🛐 Kung Fu Panda: 4.5/5⭐

Name: Kung Fu Panda 2
Review: A great sequel written with strong emotions that drives the film. Kung Fu Panda 2: 3.5/5⭐

Name: With Love
Review: With Love is a film that aims to be a feel-good experience, and at times it does succeed and falls short in creating those moments. There are moments that pay off well, mainly because those particular moments are written well. However, the main issue for me is the writing and the flow. It felt like the writing is taken for granted. There are multiple conflicts that arise for the leads, but they aren't explored deeply, instead the film just lets those conflicts fall into place on their own too conviniently. It almost felt like they just moved past it more easily than confronting them. Because of this lack of details and conviction in the writing, the impact of those feel good moments gets messed up midway. Overall, With Love worked in parts for me for its light hearted way of storytelling. With Love: 3.25/5⭐

Name: Kung Fu Panda 3
Review: Kung Fu Panda 3 being chaotic and emotional as usual, but doesn't have the impact that previous films had. Kung Fu Panda 3: 2.75/5⭐

Name: Kung Fu Panda 4
Review: A Kung Fu Panda film without the furious 5 is like eating Chicken Biryani but without Chicken. Kung Fu Panda 4: 2.5/5⭐

Name: Sarvam Maya
Review: Sarvam Maya, A film with a fresh concept and a great writing on paper that seems as good as it on the screens too. With the Humour that lands so naturally between Aju and Nivin, the film is thoroughly enjoying. Watching the film after all the hype around it are settled, Ohh Man!! that's a great Experience. One line la sollanum naa... Kumudhaa Happyy Annachiii!! Sarvam Maya: 3.75/5⭐

Name: 36 Vayadhinile
Review: "36 vayadhinile" is a simple film that's is neatly executed. The film might have its own cliched dynamics, but the Performances and the Music made it more engaging. 36 Vayadhinile: 3/5⭐

Name: Vadakkupatti Ramasamy
Review: We should actually Praise when the film is aware that it's dumb and approach the film in its own spectrum. Likewise Vadakkupatti Ramasamy(VPR) is really aware of its genre and does a good job. VPR is intentionally brainless with unintentional humour at most. Vadakkupatti Ramasamy: 3/5⭐

Name: Krishnarjuna Yudham
Review: The film is enjoyable at few moments but stretches it too far this becoming a Mediocre at best. Krishnarjuna Yudham: 2/5⭐

Name: Bhale Bhale Magadivoy
Review: A simple dish turns into delicious treat, when served with warm hospitality, and seasoned with the right toppings that perfectly complement it. Bhale Bhale Magadivoy works in a similar way. Despite having a simple plotline, it is elevated by clever situational humour, Nani’s effortless comic timing, and a narration that perfectly complements the film’s tone, making the whole experience an highly enjoyable ride. Bhale Bhale Magadivoy: 3.5/5⭐ PS: I can't believe that the man who made this also made the legendary Rajasaab too.

Name: Pilla Zamindar
Review: The film has a catchy One Liner that is told in a way which keeps the audience hooked into the film with its humour and and the Character Arc that is purely justified by Nani's performance. Pilla Zamindar: 3/5⭐

Name: Sookshmadarshini
Review: Idhu Nalla irukku... Ada Nalla irukku!!! Spoiler Theriyum ndra naala ending impact koranchiruchi but overall the film was good. Sookshmadarshini: 3/5⭐

Name: Drunken Master
Review: I have a soft spot for the retro Martial Art films and Jackie Chan as a funny young prankster is my type of genre. Drunken master: 3.5/5⭐

Name: Dhurandhar The Revenge
Review: You can even put 100 propagandas into my mind, if you can make a film like this!! Yeah, this film is full of propagandas, so what? The film justifies itself with its storytelling. With the enormous runtime of 229 mins, the film creates its own world and pulls you into it. Once the film starts, you don't feel the time passing because every scene keeps the storyboard moving and never lets you off the hook. Looking at Dhurandhar as a single story, part 1 is like Hamza performing foreplay before s*x and part 2 is Hamza going all out in action. PS: This is purely a theater material, don't miss it in big screen. Watching this in big screens really elevates the entire experience.

Name: A Taxi Driver
Review: A film that begins in light-hearted comical tone hides something that is darker in pages of korean history. Once the film gets into the dark pages, it gets more gritty and emotional for the viewer to watch it. The Narration and the Character Arc combined with the commendable performance from the leads turns this film into something more memorable and hard hitting watch. Kang Sang Ho - doesn't really act, he Lived the role. Hats off Sir. Spoiler But not Spoiler (Post film timeline): He couldn't find him till the end, because Mr Kim is dead by that time. Mr Kim has been traumatized by this incident in Ghwanju and started drinking. He died in 1984 due to liver problem. A Taxi Driver: 4/5⭐

The film had a complex plot for its time of release, but the simple narration and the foreshadowing of all the minute details with the story flow made it more easy to understand in the time of release. 

Watching it now makes me admire the writing that's went into the film. It is planned and executed perfectly down to the last scene. Suriya might have given a commendable performance, but the man writer director Vikram K Kumar stole the show.

Name: Vasool Raja MBBS
Review:
KamalHassan-CrazyMohan (KH-CM) combo has always entertained us,but this time combined with Rajkumar Hirani's style of blending emotions into a humour film made this standout in KH-CM combo.

This film was enjoyable, is enjoyable and will be enjoyable even after decades. The amount of rewatch calibre this film has is unmatchable.

Name: A Aa
Review: I would neither say this is good nor bad, I could say one thing for sure, I was entertained at instances.

Name: Sowkarpettai
Review: It is not even worth a review.

Name: Writing Doom
Review:
The short film keeps on throwing multiple beautiful (hypothetical) arguments at you back to back, that you can't keep up with it. 
It took my mind 15 mins past the film to process what they told, and they had entire conversation in just 25mins.

Name: Srimanthudu
Review: A stylish action entertainer that blends strong emotional storytelling and philanthropy with a subtle savior complex. The film blends them perfectly into its narrative, never feeling forced. Thus providing a solid commercial entertainer.

Name: Youth
Review: En da ellarume Soona Paana (Don or Dragon) aagida mudiyuma da!! The film tries to be another don template film with a matti male lead and a casual breezy first half followed by a emotional pre Climax Character Arc. But this time, it utterly misfires with that template. The film might be entertaining at instances, but for the most parts it's just flat (Ragebaiting would be the right word) moments. Not a Great start for Ken in direction. Youth:2/5 PS: 2022 la don vandhapove adichirundha ippo indha maari padam laam vandhurukadhu laa 😮‍💨😮‍💨

Name: I Don't Want to Go Back Alone
Review: The short film is slow but nice slow. I ain't used to watching gay love stories, but this is a good watch tbh.

Name: Iraivi
Review:
Everytime the men of iraivi tries to get redemption through character arc, the film shatters it and reminds that men will be men by acting out of rage without think of its consequences and the people around them.

OG Karthik Subburaj set his standards too high that he himself struggles to get past that.

Name: Arul
Review: Sun Tv days

Name: Rango
Review: Rango is chaotic, worldly, funny, western, stylish and humourous in its own way. In one line Rango feels like watching a KamalHassan-CrazyMohan film.

Name: Jana Nayagan
Review: You can say the film is mindless, logicless, Ragebait, Cringe, too commercial, political SoftLaunch, and whatever you want, but one thing that you can't say is "This is Boring". Balaiyya padam dhaan entertaining ah irukum nu paatha... avaru padam remake panna kuda entertainment guaranteed ah irukum pola. Idhu Kesari dhaan, aana mundhiri thiraatchai (political launch) thookala Iruka kesari. If you don't mind those nuts in between your dish, you can try this. Kesari and remake kesari is not par with each other, there are few moments that works better in their respective version and there are moments that don't. But one thing that the both Kesari version did is that it keeps you entertained with the typical masala and build ups. Ivlo solranaala padam super ah iruku nu nenaikadhinga... Romba Mosam nu solladha alavuku sumaar ah iruku avlo dhaan. JanaNayagan: 2/5⭐

Name: Malai Malai
Review: If not for the comedy track, this would have been a disappoint rather than a mediocre.

Name: Paiyaa
Review:
Adada Mazhaida-
Karthi pretends to like rain. Tamanna turns around enjoying rain. Karthi becomes hesitant, Tamanna turns back, Karthi pretends again. (BG la chorus start aagum) ~ Thandhaneyyy thandhananey thandhananeyyy Karthi really starts to enjoy rain... Apdiye slow ah ADADA MAZHAIDA start aagum 🤌🏼✨
Wammala bliss ra 😭🤌🏼✨ Idhella mudinchu kadaisila... Karthi frnds tamanna kita confess panna aprm Tamanna hug pannuvanga
Thuli Thuli Thuli guitar prelude start aagum "A film by N.Lingusamy" nu apdiye credits ku cut aagum BG la Thuli Thuli Thuli song play aaga... (Naan apdiye ezhundhu vibe panna start panten!!)
Yuvan on Peak STEROIDS 💉🩸

Name: Thaai Kizhavi
Review:
A dramedy genre is one of the most under used genre in tamil industry. It's not an easy task to make a dramedy cuz most of the times the comedy over shadows the drama part in the film and vice versa. But Thaai Kizhavi becomes an exception in that genre that finds the perfect blend between the comedy and the drama, which makes the film even more enjoyable.

Although Radhika mam screen time is very less, she slayed as PavunuThaayi aka Thaai Kizhavi. Even the supporting actors gave a commendable performances. 

The first half filled with laughter riots sets the tone of the film and then it gradually shifts in the second half into a serious film, still maintaining the overall humourous tone of the film. 

PavunuThaayi walking for Virumaandi song summarises the entire film. One word la sollanum naa... padam Nallaruku!!!

Name: Nala Damayanthi
Review:
A breezy light-hearted romcom from the early 2000s which pleases you even after two long decades.

Ramji and Damayanthi confessing their love for each other in the courtroom really feels like a tight warm hug.

Why can't they make a breezy romcoms like these nowadays?

Name: Tere Ishk Mein
Review:
There are 3 characters who require therapy:
1. Shankar Gurukkal
2. Mukti
3. The Audience who watched this shit.

Enna elavu da eduthu vechirukinga. Oru mannum nalla illa. Periya bhai Music mattum illana avlo dhaan.

Name: Shamitabh
Review: Shamitabh is intriguingly fresh and innovative in paper, but loses its grip on the screen and stumbles it's way to the end. The leads tried to save it with their performances, but the film stumbled way too much in execution that even their performances couldn't make it as intriguing as it is in paper.

Name: Iraivan
Review: Idha paathadhuku pesama TV ah off pannitu mathiyanam nalla thoongirukalam 🚶🏼‍♂️‍➡️🚶🏼‍♂️‍➡️

Name: Adipurush
Review: The guilty pleasure of watching shitty films knowing it is shitty is boundless.

Name: Dil To Pagal Hai
Review: Dil To Pagal Hai is a simple yet charming love triangle story from SRK classic lover boy era. The beauty of these kinda films is that you know what's gonna happen next, but still you end up smiling, and emoting yourself when that actually happens.

Name: Chak De! India
Review: Chak de India is more than a sports drama, although the film follows the typical underdog structure, it stands apart by its way of portraying the team dynamics and how they overcome the differences. This establishes the character traits of the players and make the film seem more natural yet being cinematic at places. The is not without flaws, but it strikes right on point when needed, thus making all those flaws seems invisible.

Name: Chalte Chalte
Review: Chalte Chalte follows the typical bollywood rom-com formula, and a manipulative lead. Despite following the generic cinematic romcom tropes, it still feels too artificial even for a cinematic rom-com standards. Spoiler ahead!! They would both be better if they got divorced. Not sure if this is a 1.5 or 2⭐

Name: Veer-Zaara
Review:
VeerZaara doesn't try to be a modern love, rather chooses to be a classic old school romance with patience, longing, and sacrifice being expressed in peotic way in its own sense. 

The pacing is definitely slow, but that allows the moments to breathe, thus making the emotions sustain in the hearts of the audience. Shahrukh Khan delivers one of his best performance as Veer and Pretty Zinta delivers a charming act as Zaara making the chemistry between Veer and Zaara seem more lovely and natural and making their love easy to root for.

The film may be over romanticizing sacrifice too much, and it might not be everyone cup of tea. But if you could align yourself into its world, the film tells you a tale of love that will stay deep in your heart even after the film ends.

Name: Happy Raj
Review:
Happy Raj felt like watching 1000reels sorted with the worst possible algorithm, where I only liked one tenth of them. With all of them being far to apart, by the time the moment arrives, the impact of the last scene was totally faded away.

Few things that was worth watching is "Happy Raj Facebook profile" and Sri Gowri priya Malai Malai dance and GV's mom enjoying bangalore and George Mariyan acting.

This isn't out right bad, but also not good too!!

Please idhuku aprm aachum Don template ah vitu tholainga da 😤 Mudila 😤

Name: Dilwale Dulhania Le Jayenge
Review: DDLJ is so iconic that I refined the romcom genre of bollywood and later became a genre itself. I loved the way Aditya Chopra uses the metaphorical story telling in the dialogues to enlarge the emotional impact of the scene. The film with a banger soundtrack that gives us Nostalgiaaa. I haven't watched the film before, but I have listened to people talking about how this influenced the Bollywood cinema and their memories with this film and how much love they have for this film. When I watched this for the first time today, the film really lived up to my expectations, maybe even more than that. Le Jayenge Le Jayenge Dilwale Dulhania Le Jayenge 💃🕺

Name: Groundhog Day
Review:
If this isn't the perfect Romcom, then I don't know what else is!! Everything about this film is so lovely, all the jokes that landed right in point, at the character progression of what a human would turn into if his actions has no consequences, then pursuing all those mischievous adventures and slowly realising that he is falling for the woman he once tried to play with. The moment he realises and starts to do good deeds to the people around him and getting the pleasure of doing good, and the love that blossoms between him and Rita. Phil carving Rita's face on the snow
~I know your face so well, I could carve it with my eyes closed. ~It's lovely. I don't know what to say. ~I do. No matter what happen tomorrow, or for the rest of my life, I'm happy now. Because I Love You.
Aww man!!! Sweet 🤌🏼✨

Name: Made in Korea
Review:
Made in Korea doesn't aim high, but still falls short with writing being too weak feels too artificial to believe in and high relying on VoiceOvers feels repetitive and makes it even more difficult to connect with the emotions and root for their likes.

A terrible misfire.

Name: The Sea Beast

Name: Aayirathil Oruvan
Review:
This is one of the film that I hated as a kid, and the only scene I liked back then is Karthi fight in the arena. I hated this cuz I never really understood what is happening. But once I was matured enough to understand this film, I was actually astonished by the writer director Genius SelvaRagavan. How could someone come up with a story like this and how the hell did he execute this perfectly.

The film always travelled in a thin line between fantasy and reality, making us feel surreal yet hitting us hard with the deep emotions and cultural references.

I have always thought that "war is the most cruelest" but this made me change my opinion that ~The aftermath of war is far more cruel that the war itself.

Name: Kadhal Konden
Review: Selvaraghavan explores the unexplored pages of the love in Tamil cinema with kadhal kondaen and 7G Rainbow colony. Maniratnam and GVM has showed the colourful pages of love, Selvaraghavan dared to show us the raw, unsettling dark pages of love. Tamil cinema had a new wave in 2010s, but I believe the first new wave was kicked in the early 2000s and Selva had a major strike in that new wave. A romance film will be a romance film and thriller be thriller till end, but he chose to break that rule and mix a dark psychological thriller into the romance film. The Genre shift he introduced was entirely new for the Tamil audience back then (I never knew it personally, but that's what I have been told). The confidence and the control of craft he had is unbelievable for a Debutant director. He broke many stereotypes of Tamil cinema. A hero must look good and muscular. A character should either be too good or be outrageous villain, there is no middle ground. Spoiler Ahead!! Vinod has never got the love he craved for in his childhood. The only person who treated vinod as a fellow human and cared about him is church Father. When he leaves the orphanage and gets into college, the entire people around him treates him as shit. All that he craved for is to be noticed and have someone to care for him. When Divya does it for him, he starts to develop feelings for her, while she never sees him in such a way, and falls for someone else. That's the plot for now. Selva has always treated love and lust as two different emotions. For a Character like Vinod who had a traumatic childhood experience, he never sees lust as an expression of love, rather to him lust/sex is vulgar, shameful act. When he sees Divya and Aadhi kissing each other, it makes him uncomfortable and he is never comfortable seeing them together. When he witness his friend having sex at the dome, his lustful emotions starts to kick in for the first time. And him seeing aadhi and divya daily, he mistakes the lust is love. Then when he elopes with Divya (abducted her, but she didn't know it), He tries to talk in with Divya about how he can replace aadhi in her life, Divya assures him that aadhi is her lover and vinod is her friend, reminding him that they are different (She mistakes him of asking this out of his innocence). Divya had such a trust on Vinod, that she asked him to turn back, so that she could change her dress. Vinod is turning back and he is in a moral ambiguity from his lustful feelings for her and not break the trust she had on him. With Yuvan background score and Dhanush acting combined, this scene goes to a another level of tension building making the audience to root that please don't look back at her. Later on night, he tries to get into her bed which wakes divya, she never gets uncomfortable and question his intention of getting into the bed with her, rather she asks him to lay on help lap and enquires about the wounds on his body. Then Vinod opens up about his childhood trauma, hearing it Divya cries and comforts him with her words and treats him as a child. Hearing her words of assurance, and the blind trust she has on him, that she never judged him for getting into her bed bareback, He dumps all the lustful thoughts about Divya. When Divya is told all the truth about Vinod, she never believes it and defends him. And When she witness the psychopatic nature of Vinod, he abducts her again and confess his love for her. Divya still seeing him as friend, rejects him. This hits him with reality and triggers his fear of losing Divya from his life. Then later when Divya holds Vinod and Aadhi's hands on a cliff, Aadhi insists Divya to leave Vinod's hands, Divya refuses to leave anyone. Vinod understands that Divya has always loved him and Aadhi equally and his fear of losing Divya forever vanishes. With that happy thought Vinod leaves Divya's hands and falls into the cliff with a smile. Ohh Man, What a film !! I have always watched the trimmed version of this in television. But this time I get to watch the original theatrical cut. I don't know why I narrated the entire story, but I know for sure that I was so astonished by the original cut of film in such a way, that I don't know what I should write in my review. I thought of going in the flow and ended up writing the entire plot instead (Sorry but not sorry!). PEAK DIRECTION! PEAK WRITING!! PEAK MUSIC!! PEAK ACTING!! Dhanush - Selvaraghavan - Yuvan 🛐 - 🛐 - 🛐 Selva - You GENIUS!! You are so daring enough to choose this for you debut subject. Kadhal Kondaen!! This is how three young studs made a revolution in Tamil cinema.

Name: Anjathe
Review:
Anjathey is one of the underrated masterpieces of Tamil cinema that deserved to be celebrated. I once stated the original new wave of Tamil cinema begun in early and mid 2000s. Selvaraghavan, Mysskin, Ram and Ameer sultan were one of the prominent names who redefined the structure of commercial cinema with their more rooted storytelling and the protagonist who is vulnerable to all kind of emotions (basically a humane figure). Their films reminded us that heroes are also humans and they too have their bad and good sides. The character arc plays a major role in their films.

Here Mysskin took a generic one line and developed it into a detailed crime thriller with a narrative that develops with the character arc. Anjathey gets deeper with the character writing that depicts friendship, betrayal, moral ambiguity that conflicts with the actions of the character. All those combined with the perfect three arc structural narration. A strong setup of the story with character relationships, morality and aspirations, followed by the confrontation of the betrayal, Crime world, investigation and moral ambiguity. Then the final Revelation of the truth, guilt and the unavoidable consequences of the past actions that ends in the emotionally raw and rooted climax.

The next great thing about anjathey is casting the perfect choice for each role from Satya, Kiruba and Logu. Casting Padiarajan as a negative character and making people believe in his view is one of the most daring decision by Mysskin.

Anjathey is literally a masterpiece from the Tamil Cinema-vin Kurosawa!!

Name: Mouna Raagam

Story Screenplay dialogue direction - Maniratnam, kadaisiya dhaan vandhaaru Vinayak - Isai Ilaiyaraaja 🛐 Raaja's soundtrack and songs for Mounaraagam is undeniable fantastic and loveable music. Actually back then when I was a kid, I am someone who hated old songs. I would only listen to songs that releases in the past 2 years, but as time passed I grew up and slowly started listening to old songs and admiring the beauty in it. And from what I remember... Nilave Vaa was the first song that I admired consciously for beautiful lyrics and music. I even watched Mounaraagam just to listen to it. Tags: rewatch, raaja's birthday run, prime video, classic, unforgettable

Name: Paint Drying
Review:
Happy Birthday RAAJA ❤️‍🩹. I'm writing this to recommend an underrated masterpiece album by Ilaiyaraaja — How to Name It? This is one of the best albums that I have listened to in my entire life. Raaja once said that every chord and every minor has its own emotion. I never really understood that when I heard that statement for the first time. But after listening to this album, I would never deny it. Raaja strikes the right emotions with every chord, and each one hits deep in its own way. I have heard a saying that people relive their entire life just before their death. This album captures that feeling perfectly. This album is so perfect that it never feels like a collection of songs. Rather, it feels like a journey of life itself. It starts by opening the deepest parts of your brain and segments of your memories. At first, you're flooded with happy memories. Slowly, the tempo increases and you begin revisiting every moment of your life in flashes. Then comes the crash. You're left alone with your thoughts. The silence in your thoughts continues for few minutes. After that, it feels like you're listening to a conversation between your soul and the music. It started as a conversation but slowly becomes a confrontation of regrets, past and questions and then finally turns into consolation. The music heals your wounds, eases your pain, and slowly lifts your soul out of your body. Then begins the journey of solace. Your soul starts travelling through multiple dimensions at incredible speed. At first, it feels like it's searching for something. But slowly, it realizes that happiness isn't in the destination; it's in the journey itself. As the tempo settles down, the soul slows down too. You begin enjoying every moment of the journey, witnessing different forms of life across the universe. Then the soul reaches the gates of heaven. The gates slowly open and welcome it. For the first time, the soul finds complete peace. And by the time the final track ends, it feels like the soul has finally attained the long-awaited solace it had been searching for all along. I once recommended this album to my friend,and guess what she said after listening it
"This is psychedelic and divine."
I might be an atheist, but I would completely agree with that statement. This album is pure divinity. I genuinely want everyone to experience this feeling through this album. Do give it a try. I'm sure you won't regret it. And if you do listen to it, I would love to hear your thoughts about this. Have a safe interdimensional journey. 🧘🏻‍♂️✨ Raaja's Birthday Run #5

Name: Echo of existence
Review:
The film captures the true essence of the loss and beauty of relationships. The usage of the center frames tells a story of its own.

The center frame depicts arul keeping distance from the other characters and being entangled into loneliness, but when the kid arrives into the world of arul, arul and the kid both shares the same center space, and arul starts to move a little away from center. Which signifies the loneliness is being reduced. And at last when arul says "poi vilaiyadu" and moves to the end of the screen or away from the center space, but hes in a dilemma and comes back to the center space and says "Nalaiku ennala vara mudiyuma nu therila" cuts to the kid in center space and he replies "adhella enaku theriyadhu nee vara..". Arul remembers his uncle's words and cuts to the stool and rope being faded away mentioning that arul got his reasons to live (The Kid).

IDK if the filmmaker made this concious decision or not, but it's what I felt. Either way Padam pakka.

There isn't much that changed in the past year since the last rewatch. From What kinda man my father is, to the way he care for our family. Nothing changed!! But the watching all the effort my dad puts in for our family at his age, makes me respect him much more day by day. 

Last year I wanna say "I love you dad!!", and I still couldn't do it, but atleast I wished him a happy father's day today. And hopefully I could say "I love you dad" someday without this reluctance stopping me. No matter what, I would love you with my full heart dad.

Name: Vasool Raja MBBS
Review:
The film somehow gets better with every rewatch!! And this time, I owe this to Neha and her well crafted review. If it wasn't for her review, I would have probably watched this film as I always do. But after reading her review, I kept thinking... What's that the film has, that I'm missing all these years, to experience it the way she does. The film does have quirky word play, back to back humour and emotions that lands right on point, But this time I started looking at it, instead of just watching it. The film has plenty of great moments, but let me mention the two lines that stayed in my head.
"Calm ah irukuvara... Coma nu solrangale" "Vambu sonna kadhoda ninnudum... Anbu sonna moolai varaikum ettum."
How does someone casually write lines like this and just move on to the next scene 😭!? The way she expressed her love for this film made me think about how these moments would've made her feel while writing that review. Somehow, I kept going back to her words whenever those scenes comes up. And because of that, I started noticing all the tiny things that I had ignored in my previous watches. And Guess what!? I ended up feeling all the emotions she talked about. It's funny how a review can change the way you experience the film. As she said the film doesn't change, Maybe our perception of life did. And within all the laughter and hard hitting Climax, the film reminded me once again that...
Life isn't always about winning. It's about Living.
And thanks to Neha for letting me have this great experience.
Tags: youtube, rewatch, peak cinema, unforgettable, classic

Name: Mentalese Express
Review: Two people misreading each other and suffering from awkward silence is so cruel and cute at the same time.

Name: Summer Bus
Review: Kindness doesn't cost anything, so why be a miser with it? Some films are just beautiful the way they are, and this is one such.

Name: Scandal Makers
Review: A feel good drama that doesn't get heavy with emotions and stays light with humour.

Name: Marakkave Ninaikiren
Review:
A 34 minutes short film that perfectly captures the essence of Why - How
மது நாட்டுக்கும், வீட்டுக்கும் கேடு!! மது உணர்வையும், உறவையும் உடைக்கும்!!
This reminded me of a thirukkural எற்றென்று இரங்குவ செய்யற்க செய்வானேல் மற்றன்ன செய்யாமை நன்று. Which means, never commit a sin that we would regret later, if done, the best response is to never commit them again. I'm so proud to say that I never had/never will consume alcohol in my life. And films like this makes me stay strong in that decision.

Name: Oru Oorla Oru Rajakumari
Review:
The best thing about bhagyaraj sir's films is that they have multiple side plots running parallel to the main plot, and he slowly converges them altogether with his screenplay making the film form a complete circle. No wonder why he is considered screenplay king back then!!

May your soul Rest in peace Bhagyaraj Sir.

Name: Front of the Class
Review: Films that radiates hope and comfort are definitely my cup of tea.

Name: Nayagan Nayagi
Review:
A well written, directed film, but can't say what let down, but something was definitely feeling off. Maybe the problem was with my high expectations after watching Ini and Markkave Ninaikiren, even a decent film feels like a mediocre!!

This is still a good watch btw.

Name: Obsession
Review:
It felt so freaking weird that I ain't even sure of how I feel right now. But I'm damn sure that I was thrilled, enjoyed and freaked out watching this.
Everytime I see Nikki on screen, I felt the tension in me. The actor who played Nikki deserves more than a praise for her undeniably great performance.
